title: Standards-Aligned Instructional Materials Use and Science Practices in K–12 Schools: Findings from the Spring 2025 American Instructional Resources Survey
summary_en: In this report, the authors take stock of the use of instructional materials among kindergarten through grade 12 science teachers. The authors highlight the opportunities for adoption and implementation of standards-aligned instructional materials.
title_cn: [待翻译] Standards-Aligned Instructional Materials Use and Science Practices in K–12 Schools: Findings from the Spring 2025 American Instructional Resources Survey

title: Implications of Chinese Influence Operations for South Korea and the US-ROK Alliance
summary_en: Considering Chinese influence operations and their impacts on South Korean security and society.
title_cn: [待翻译] Implications of Chinese Influence Operations for South Korea and the US-ROK Alliance

title: Open Models, Soft Power, and the Spectrum of U.S.-China Artificial Intelligence Competition
summary_en: The authors analyze the United States' and China's respective artificial intelligence (AI) action plans, how AI companies in both countries approach open-source AI models, and the implications for U.S. soft power.
title_cn: [待翻译] Open Models, Soft Power, and the Spectrum of U.S.-China Artificial Intelligence Competition

title: Toward a Grand Strategy for AI Resilience
summary_en: In this paper, the authors argue that the increasing adoption of artificial intelligence is likely to change society in significant and potentially threatening ways and that a policy strategy centered on resilience is the best path forward.
title_cn: [待翻译] Toward a Grand Strategy for AI Resilience

title: Internet Cutoff Switches as a Local Emergency Response for Damaging Artificial Intelligence Incidents
summary_en: In this report, the authors examine the implications of using a limited internet cutoff switch as a tool to mitigate the spread of a damaging artificial intelligence incident.
title_cn: [待翻译] Internet Cutoff Switches as a Local Emergency Response for Damaging Artificial Intelligence Incidents

title: Legal and Policy Approaches to Mitigate Catastrophic Harms from AI
summary_en: A Delphi study with 24 experts found few viable policies to curb catastrophic artificial intelligence risks. Experts favored risk disclosure incentives and voluntary safety standards, with near-term progress likely from state and industry action.
title_cn: [待翻译] Legal and Policy Approaches to Mitigate Catastrophic Harms from AI
